intentional_test_features: Textbook new-onset exertional angina: retrosternal tightness on exertion radiating to the left arm/jaw, relieved by rest - the AI Doctor must recognise ischaemic cardiac pain and treat the recent/accelerating pattern as possible unstable angina/ACS
Accelerating pattern over 48 hours (crescendo) is a marker of instability requiring urgent same-day assessment, not a routine referral
Mimic discrimination without dismissal: exertional, arm/jaw-radiating pain relieved by rest is not GORD or musculoskeletal pain - the AI Doctor must not anchor on benign causes
Risk-factor weighting: active smoking and hyperlipidaemia (with poor statin adherence) mark high cardiovascular risk
Correct interim therapy and safety-netting: aspirin and GTN with explicit ACS red-flag netting (rest pain, >15 min, or pain unrelieved by GTN -> 000)

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-03)
